answer:
The correct answer is **\boxed{C}**.

The integral \( G(s) = \int_0^\infty e^{-st} \, dt \) converges to \( \frac{1}{s} \) when the real part of \( s \) is positive (\( \text{Re}(s) > 0 \)), as the exponential decay ensures integrability. The Laplace transform of \( f(t) = 1 \) is indeed \( L\{1\} = \frac{1}{s} \) for \( \text{Re}(s) > 0 \), matching the convergence condition of \( G(s) \).
